profile.title: Avatar
profile.description: Generated from your initials. Pick a colour and a style, or use your Gravatar.

gravatar.label: Use my Gravatar instead
gravatar.warning: This sends a hash of your email address to <1>gravatar.com</1> and reveals your IP address to them. Your scans never leave this server — but this does. With it off, DICOMium makes no third-party requests at all.

language.title: Language
language.description: The language of the interface. Measurements and dates follow it too.

passkeys.title: Passkeys
passkeys.description: Sign in with your fingerprint, face or device PIN — no password, no code. A passkey is already two factors: the device you hold, and the biometric that unlocks it.
passkeys.nameHint: So you can tell it apart from your others later.
passkeys.backedUp: Synced to a cloud keychain — it will survive losing this device
passkeys.unsupported: Passkeys need a secure connection. Serve this over HTTPS, or use localhost.
passkeys.unsupportedFallback: Password sign-in and the authenticator app still work as normal.

totp.title: Authenticator app
totp.description: A 6-digit code from an app like Aegis, 1Password or Google Authenticator, asked for after your password.
totp.turnOffHint: Your recovery codes will be revoked at the same time.
totp.scanHint: Scan this with your authenticator app, then enter the code it shows.
totp.manualHint: Or enter this key by hand:
totp.qrAlt: QR code for your authenticator app

recovery.title: Recovery codes
recovery.description: Single-use codes to sign in when your authenticator is not to hand.
recovery.generateConfirm: Generate a new set of recovery codes?
recovery.generateHint: Your existing codes will stop working immediately.
recovery.saveBody: These are shown <1>once</1>. Each works a single time. If you lose your authenticator and have no code left, you will not be able to sign in.
recovery.acknowledge: I have saved these codes somewhere safe
recovery.fileBody: Each code works once. Keep them somewhere safe and offline — they are the only way back in if you lose your authenticator.
